title: Dogfooding Review: Compact Output And Proof Recommendation Overreach
goal: Review whether package-selected compact outputs and proof recommendations are still making agents inspect or override more context than the current task needs.
scope: Recent dogfooding of startup, proof, planning summary, and GitHub issue intake flows on 2026-05-07.
Friction observed while closing local-only startup and proof ergonomics work.
non_goals: Redesign proof routing in this review artifact.
Change active TypeScript command-generation implementation scope.

review_mode.review question: Do compact workflow outputs and proof recommendations reliably give the smallest trustworthy next action?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: agentic-workspace start --profile tiny, summary --profile compact, recent commits 70a12a25 and 85f29f0d, current planning state

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-workspace start --profile tiny --task ... --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --profile compact --task ... --format json; git status --short
review_method.evidence sources: Current thread dogfooding observations; .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml; GitHub issues #826 #828; commits 70a12a25 and 85f29f0d

references.label: promoted compact routing and proof recommendation friction
repo-local invocation drift in generated companion tools
commit hook and proof routing tightening

findings.title: Compact routing still overexposes unrelated active-plan pressure for friction intake tasks
findings.summary: The task-scoped compact summary for prioritizing friction-related lanes still returned a TypeScript active-plan next action even though the user asked for review creation, issue promotion, and roadmap ordering. This did not block progress, but it forced manual interpretation of routing output.
findings.evidence: On 2026-05-07, `uv run agentic-workspace summary --profile compact --task "prioritize friction-related lanes" --format json` reported the active TypeScript execplan as the recommended next action while the startup router had correctly identified planning-review promotion skills for the user task.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents can spend tokens inspecting or following unrelated active execution context before completing friction intake, and may over-trust broad proof or routing recommendations when a smaller planning-only action is intended.
findings.suggested action: Add a compact-output/proof-recommendation improvement lane that sharpens task-scoped routing when review, issue-intake, and roadmap-prioritization language is present, including an explicit reason when unrelated active-plan pressure is intentionally shown.
findings.promotion target: GitHub review issue and roadmap candidate
findings.promotion trigger: Promote now because the user explicitly asked to create issues from the general-improvement cases and prioritize friction-related lanes.
findings.post-remediation note shape: Intent / What changed / Proof / Remaining risk / Durable residue / Next owner

recommendation.promote: Promote the finding to a review issue and place it near the top of friction-related roadmap candidates.
recommendation.defer: Implementation should remain deferred behind any friction lane that blocks safe proof or checked-in invocation.
recommendation.dismiss: Do not dismiss; the finding is current and repeatable from compact routing output.

retention.closeout shape: Keep this artifact as compact evidence for the issue and roadmap candidate.
retention.trigger: Archive or distill after the promoted issue lands and the compact route produces a smaller next action for friction-intake tasks.
retention.proof surface: Planning surface checks plus a focused compact-summary fixture or command proof.
retention.source retention rule: Store decision, confidence, source refs, and reopen/promote triggers; do not copy issue titles, current status snapshots, long command output, or source summaries unless the copied text is the judgment itself.
retention.distillation path: When decisions are promoted into issues, planning state, Memory, docs, contracts, or checks, keep this artifact as evidence/history and remove or summarize reconstructable source context at the next cleanup.

drift_log: 2026-05-07: Review created from dogfooding friction and promoted to GitHub issue #830.
2026-05-07: #830 implemented in friction-lane pass with task-scoped compact summary routing preferring matched roadmap candidates for friction-lane tasks.
